title: Prevent repeated curator-contact handoff selection after current packet delivery
summary: Patched the distribution lane selector so a curator-contact handoff packet that is already current and already delivered in the active review window is treated as in-flight work instead of a fresh action slot. This stops the loop from rediscovering the same manual-contact packet and lets the next run advance into a real new lane.
repair_summary: Added selector logic that detects when the current manual-contact target set already matches curator_contact_handoff_packet_latest.md.
Required a recent successful curator_contact_handoff_packet_execution before treating that packet as current, so undelivered packets can still be selected normally.
Added a regression test proving the selector falls through to distribution_reset instead of reselecting curator_contact_handoff_packet once the current packet is already delivered.
Refactored the new regression test to use ExitStack so the test file stays under Python's static nested-block limit.
expected_effect: Future marketing runs should stop burning the best action slot on an already-delivered manual-contact packet and should move into genuinely new demand-capture or reset lanes during the same saturated review window.
